notes: This registry documents the currently implemented deterministic production logic. It is not a literature-complete research catalog.
Formula definitions below describe what is actually in Python today, including source-unit caveats.
Production approval remains provisional because survivorship bias and point-in-time fundamental cleanliness are not solved in V1.

factors.rebalance_frequency: daily ranking; consumed by monthly rebalance in backtest and scheduled runtime pipeline
factors.lag_rule: Uses trailing history only. Excludes the most recent 21 trading days from the 12-month lookback.
factors.intuition: Captures medium-term relative strength while avoiding the very recent month, which is often noisy or mean-reverting.
known_failure_modes: momentum crash risk during sharp regime reversal
can chase crowded leaders late in a trend
sensitive to static seed universe and missing delisted names

factors.lag_rule: Uses trailing 30 trading days of adjusted-close returns only; no forward information.
factors.intuition: Prefers names with smoother recent price behavior. In the current alpha composition, lower realized volatility ranks better.
known_failure_modes: low volatility can become expensive and crowded
can underweight fast winners with deservedly high volatility
volatility regime shifts can make recent calm misleading

factors.python_definition: 1.0 if last_close is not None and sma(close_series, 200) is not None and last_close > sma_200 else 0.0
factors.lag_rule: Uses trailing close history through the evaluation date only.
factors.intuition: Acts as a simple trend filter so strong momentum names below their long-term trend get less support.
known_failure_modes: slow to react after major bottoms
binary threshold can overstate tiny differences around SMA(200)

factors.formula_latex: Q(t) = \mathbb{1}[\text{ticker passes deterministic CORE quality screen}]
factors.rebalance_frequency: daily ranking input; thresholded quality screen is shared with CORE eligibility
factors.lag_rule: Depends on the latest available fundamental snapshot. Not yet point-in-time clean in V1.
factors.intuition: Gives a modest bonus to names that meet the current deterministic quality screen, helping CORE-eligible names rank better without fully dominating the tactical signal.
known_failure_modes: quality result depends heavily on source field definitions and units
non-point-in-time fundamentals can create hindsight contamination in research

factors.python_definition: alpha_score = round((0.5 * momentum_rank) + (0.2 * vol_rank) + (0.15 * trend_bonus) + (0.15 * quality_bonus), 6)
factors.rebalance_frequency: daily ranking artifact used by runtime strategist and backtest
factors.lag_rule: All subcomponents are trailing-only in code, but the quality input inherits V1 fundamental data limitations.
factors.intuition: Combines medium-term momentum, preference for lower recent volatility, trend confirmation, and a modest fundamental quality bonus into a single deterministic ranking score.
known_failure_modes: relative ranks on a tiny static universe can exaggerate differences
weights are heuristic rather than deeply validated
quality bonus can become ineffective if the quality screen is too harsh or data fields are poorly normalized

contextual_non_scoring_inputs.intuition: Recorded for context and reporting; not directly weighted in alpha_score_v1.
known_failure_modes: false overbought/oversold readings during persistent trends
easy to misuse as a standalone timing signal
